title: Children and composition

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Children and composition; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Learn and Reference; React 19.2; provisions applicable to Children and composition
sources.claim: children is a prop containing nested renderable content and enables wrapper, layout, and compound composition.

canonicalExplanation.summary: children is a prop containing nested renderable content and enables wrapper, layout, and compound composition.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A Card renders its children inside semantic article structure without assuming their concrete type. Your goal is to design and implement a reusable composition boundary for varied child content. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Components and JSX; Props and one-way data flow; JSX expressions and attributes. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: children is a prop containing nested renderable content and enables wrapper, layout, and compound composition. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Children may be absent, one node, or many; cloning them couples implementation and does not make ownership transfer. This draft therefore rejects: children is always an array and can be indexed safely.

includes: Compose layout and behavior through children or focused slots rather than prop explosions.
Design and implement a reusable composition boundary for varied child content.
excludes: Children may be absent, one node, or many; cloning them couples implementation and does not make ownership transfer.

outcomes: Design and implement a reusable composition boundary for varied child content.

examples.title: Children and composition: concrete case
examples.situation: A Card renders its children inside semantic article structure without assuming their concrete type.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A Card renders its children inside semantic article structure without assuming their concrete type.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: children is an opaque renderable value; use supported React helpers only when traversal is actually required.
examples.result: The result follows because children is an opaque renderable value; use supported React helpers only when traversal is actually required.

examples.title: Children and composition: boundary case
examples.situation: Children may be absent, one node, or many; cloning them couples implementation and does not make ownership transfer.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Children may be absent, one node, or many; cloning them couples implementation and does not make ownership transfer.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: children is always an array and can be indexed safely.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: children is an opaque renderable value; use supported React helpers only when traversal is actually required.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete React: Component-Based Browser Applications artifact to explain why this belief fails: children is always an array and can be indexed safely.

analogy.example: A state-driven component workshop can model Children and composition, where inputs and state produce a UI description.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Children and composition before using exact platform terms
limitations: A workshop does not reproduce reconciliation identity, hook ordering, concurrent rendering, effects, or browser accessibility APIs.

recap.summary: You should now be able to design and implement a reusable composition boundary for varied child content. Keep this boundary in view: Children may be absent, one node, or many; cloning them couples implementation and does not make ownership transfer.

recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “Component API design”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
